revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=永山古墳| 所在地=大阪府堺市堺区永山園| 位置=| 形状=前方後円墳| 築造年代=5世紀（推定）| 規模=墳丘長104ｍ| 被葬者=| 史跡指定=| 出土品=| 画像|}}
'''永山古墳'''（ながやまこふん）とは、大阪府堺市堺区永山園に所在する[[前方後円墳]]。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]に属しており、[[宮内庁]]より大仙陵古墳の[[陪塚]]の指定を受けている。
== 概要== 大仙古墳の[[周濠]]の北側に接して、前方部を南南西に向けて立地している前方後円墳である。大仙陵古墳の陪塚の可能性がある。墳丘は段築によって築かれ、全長104ｍ、後円部は径約63ｍ、高さ9ｍ、前方部幅67ｍ、高さ約8ｍを測る。西側くびれ部に造出しが認められ、馬蹄形の周濠がめぐる。内部主体、副葬品は不明である。現在、宮内庁より大仙陵古墳（百舌鳥耳原中陵）の陪塚として管理されている。
==参考文献 == *松村 隆文 「永山古墳」『日本古墳大辞典』 株式会社東京堂出版 1989年 428項
[[category:古墳]] [[category:前方後円墳]] [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:堺市の歴史]] [[category:堺区]]

revision.comment: 周濠の堺市指定史跡への指定に係る事柄について加筆。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=永山古墳| 所属=[[百舌鳥古墳群]]| 所在地=大阪府堺市堺区東永山園| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|scale:20000}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 築造年代=5世紀（推定）| 規模=墳丘長104ｍ| 被葬者=| 史跡指定=[[宮内庁]]治定（「百舌鳥耳原中陵」の陪冢）
堺市指定史跡「永山古墳周濠」| 出土品=| 画像|}} '''永山古墳'''（ながやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]東永山園に所在する[[前方後円墳]]。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。 [[宮内庁]]により第16代[[仁徳天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]の「百舌鳥耳原中陵（もずのみみはらのなかのみささぎ）」の[[陪冢]]に治定されているほか、周濠は堺市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要== 大仙古墳の[[周濠]]の北側に接して、前方部を南南西に向けて立地している前方後円墳である。大仙陵古墳の陪塚の可能性がある。墳丘は段築によって築かれ、全長104ｍ、後円部は径約63ｍ、高さ9ｍ、前方部幅67ｍ、高さ約8ｍを測る。西側くびれ部に[[造出し]]が認められ、馬蹄形の周濠がめぐる。内部主体、副葬品は不明である。現在、宮内庁より大仙陵古墳（百舌鳥耳原中陵）の陪塚として管理されている。 == 文化財 == === 堺市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 永山古墳周濠 - 平成28年4月11日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/shisei/koho/hodo/hodoteikyoshiryo/0411_02.files/0411_2.pdf 堺市報道提供資料 平成28年4月11日提供]}}（堺市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} ==参考文献 == *松村隆文 「永山古墳」『日本古墳大辞典』 株式会社東京堂出版 1989年 428項 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/nagayama.html デジタル古墳百科 > 古墳データベース > 永山古墳]（堺市ウェブサイト） *[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/city/_rekibun/mozu/map/index2.html#m2 仁徳天皇陵古墳周辺｜百舌鳥古墳群]（堺市ウェブサイト） *[http://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/spot.php?id=120 スポット詳細：永山古墳｜堺観光ガイド] {{デフォルトソート:なかやまこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:堺区の歴史]] [[category:百舌鳥古墳群]] [[category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:陵墓陪冢]]

{{日本の古墳| 名称=永山古墳| 画像=[[ファイル:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
墳丘全景（手前に前方部、右奥に後円部）| 所属=[[百舌鳥古墳群]]| 所在地=大阪府堺市堺区東永山園| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|scale:20000}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 築造年代=5世紀（推定）| 規模=墳丘長104ｍ| 被葬者=| 陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定（「百舌鳥耳原中陵」の陪冢）| 史跡=堺市指定史跡「永山古墳周濠」| 出土品=| 画像|}} '''永山古墳'''（ながやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]東永山園に所在する[[前方後円墳]]。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。 [[宮内庁]]により第16代[[仁徳天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]の「[[百舌鳥耳原中陵]]（もずのみみはらのなかのみささぎ）」の陪冢に治定されているほか、周濠は堺市指定史跡に指定されている。 現在、宮内庁より大仙陵古墳（百舌鳥耳原中陵）の陪塚として管理されている。 == 文化財 == === 堺市指定文化財 === *史跡 **永山古墳周濠 - 2016年（平成28年）4月11日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/shisei/koho/hodo/hodoteikyoshiryo/0411_02.files/0411_2.pdf 堺市報道提供資料 平成28年4月11日提供]}}（堺市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == *松村隆文 「永山古墳」『日本古墳大辞典』 株式会社東京堂出版 1989年 428項 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/nagayama.html デジタル古墳百科 > 古墳データベース > 永山古墳]（堺市ウェブサイト） *[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/city/_rekibun/mozu/map/index2.html#m2 仁徳天皇陵古墳周辺｜百舌鳥古墳群]（堺市ウェブサイト） *[http://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/spot.php?id=120 スポット詳細：永山古墳｜堺観光ガイド] {{デフォルトソート:なかやまこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:堺区の歴史]] [[category:百舌鳥古墳群]] [[category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:陵墓陪冢]]

revision.comment: 追記
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=永山古墳| 画像=[[ファイル:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
墳丘全景（手前に前方部、右奥に後円部）| 所属=[[百舌鳥古墳群]]| 所在地=大阪府堺市堺区東永山園| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|scale:20000}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 築造年代=5世紀（推定）| 規模=墳丘長100ｍ| 被葬者=| 陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定（「百舌鳥耳原中陵」の陪冢）| 史跡=堺市指定史跡「永山古墳周濠」| 出土品=| 画像|}} '''永山古墳'''（ながやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]東永山園に所在する[[前方後円墳]]。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つで、[[文化遺産 (世界遺産)|世界文化遺産]] [[百舌鳥・古市古墳群|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-]] の構成資産の一部として登録されている。 [[宮内庁]]により第16代[[仁徳天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]の「[[大仙陵古墳|百舌鳥耳原中陵]]」の陪冢に治定されているほか、周濠は堺市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[大仙陵古墳]]（百舌鳥耳原中陵）の北西約50メートルにあり、前方部を南に向けた前方後円墳である{{Sfn|古墳群|2014|p=14}}。墳丘の規模は、長さ100メートル、後円部径63メートル、後円部高さ10.3メートル、前方部幅68.5メートル、前方部高さ10.3メートル。墳丘西側のくびれ部分に造り出しがあり、葺石と埴輪があり{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=永山古墳　【世界文化遺産 構成資産】|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=堺市役所文化観光局博物館学芸課}}、墳丘は2段に見えるが、他の古墳よりも腰高であるため3段築成の可能性もある{{Sfn|古墳群|2014|p=14}}。調査されていないため、構造の詳細は不明で、副葬品の内容や性格も分かっていないが、5世紀前半の築造と考えられる。周囲には濠があり、くびれ部の東側には濠をまたぐ渡り土手があるが、築造当時から存在するものかどうかは不明である{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=永山古墳／国指定文化財等データベース|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=文化庁}}。1928年（昭和3年）に前方部西端の堤上から円筒埴輪（埴輪列の可能性あり）が出土したとされる{{Sfn|古墳群|2014|p=14}}。2012年（平成24年）に管理用柵の設置工事の事前調査が行われ、東側の濠の範囲を確認し、周濠の肩は近世以降に改変を受けたことが判明。また、東側の周濠の堆積層の下層に永山古墳の遺構が良好に残されている可能性があることが確認されている{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/nagayama.html|title=永山古墳周濠|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=堺市役所文化観光局文化部文化財課}}。前方後円墳の墳端および周濠が良好に残され、隣接する大仙陵古墳（百舌鳥耳原中陵）より先に築かれた可能性が高いと考えられている。大仙陵古墳（百舌鳥耳原中陵）の陪塚として宮内庁により管理されているが、形態や規模が大きいいことから独立の古墳であると考えられている。江戸時代には「長山塚」、「長山」とよばれ、また[[王仁]]や、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の墓とする伝承もある{{Sfn|古墳群|2014|p=14}}。周濠の南側に接して[[大阪府道2号大阪中央環状線]]が周濠の一部をまたぐように通る。 == 規模 == 古墳の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長100メートル * 後円部直径63メートル。高さ10.3メートル。 * 前方部幅68.5メートル。高さ10.3メートル。 == 文化財 == === 堺市指定史跡 === * 指定名称：'''永山古墳周濠''' - 2016年（平成28年）4月11日指定。 == 世界遺産 == [[2019年]]（令和元年）7月6日に世界文化遺産 '''百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-''' を構成する百舌鳥古墳群の構成資産の一部として登録されている{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=世界遺産／文化遺産オンライン|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=文化庁}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=世界遺産／百舌鳥・古市古墳群―古代日本の墳墓群／国指定文化財等データベース|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=文化庁}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/learn/mozu_furuichi_mozu_list.html|title=百舌鳥エリア古墳リスト／百舌鳥・古市古墳群|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=百舌鳥・古市古墳群世界遺産保存活用会議}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 松村隆文 「永山古墳」『日本古墳大辞典』 株式会社東京堂出版 1989年 428項 * {{Cite book|和書|title=百舌鳥古墳群 -堺の文化財- 第7版|year=2014|publisher=堺市文化観光局文化部文化財課|author=堺市文化観光局文化部文化財課 編集|ref={{SfnRef|古墳群|2014}}}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html 永山古墳【世界文化遺産 構成資産】] - 堺市文化観光局博物館学芸課 * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html 百舌鳥古墳群リスト（詳細ページ）] - 堺市文化観光局文化部世界遺産課 * [http://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/spot.php?id=120 スポット詳細：永山古墳｜堺観光ガイド] - （公社）堺観光コンベンション協会 {{デフォルトソート:なかやまこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:堺区の歴史]] [[category:百舌鳥古墳群]] [[category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:陵墓陪冢]]

{{日本の古墳| 名称=永山古墳| 画像=[[ファイル:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
墳丘全景（手前に前方部、右奥に後円部）| 所属=[[百舌鳥古墳群]]| 所在地=大阪府堺市堺区東永山園| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=永山古墳}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 築造年代=5世紀（推定）| 規模=墳丘長100ｍ| 被葬者=| 陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定（「百舌鳥耳原中陵」の陪冢）| 史跡=堺市指定史跡「永山古墳周濠」| 出土品=| 画像||地図2={{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=240|marker=place-of-worship}}{{Location map|Japan Osaka#Japan|float=center}}}} '''永山古墳'''（ながやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]東永山園に所在する[[前方後円墳]]。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つで、[[文化遺産 (世界遺産)|世界文化遺産]] [[百舌鳥・古市古墳群|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-]] の構成資産の一部として登録されている。 [[宮内庁]]により第16代[[仁徳天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]の「[[大仙陵古墳|百舌鳥耳原中陵]]」の陪冢に治定されているほか、周濠は堺市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[大仙陵古墳]]（百舌鳥耳原中陵）の北西約50メートルにあり、前方部を南に向けた前方後円墳である{{Sfn|古墳群|2014|p=14}}。墳丘の規模は、長さ100メートル、後円部径63メートル、後円部高さ10.3メートル、前方部幅68.5メートル、前方部高さ10.3メートル。墳丘西側のくびれ部分に造り出しがあり、葺石と埴輪があり{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=永山古墳　【世界文化遺産 構成資産】|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=堺市役所文化観光局博物館学芸課}}、墳丘は2段に見えるが、他の古墳よりも腰高であるため3段築成の可能性もある{{Sfn|古墳群|2014|p=14}}。調査されていないため、構造の詳細は不明で、副葬品の内容や性格も分かっていないが、5世紀前半の築造と考えられる。周囲には濠があり、くびれ部の東側には濠をまたぐ渡り土手があるが、築造当時から存在するものかどうかは不明である{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=永山古墳／国指定文化財等データベース|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=文化庁}}。1928年（昭和3年）に前方部西端の堤上から円筒埴輪（埴輪列の可能性あり）が出土したとされる{{Sfn|古墳群|2014|p=14}}。2012年（平成24年）に管理用柵の設置工事の事前調査が行われ、東側の濠の範囲を確認し、周濠の肩は近世以降に改変を受けたことが判明。また、東側の周濠の堆積層の下層に永山古墳の遺構が良好に残されている可能性があることが確認されている{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/nagayama.html|title=永山古墳周濠|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=堺市役所文化観光局文化部文化財課}}。前方後円墳の墳端および周濠が良好に残され、隣接する大仙陵古墳（百舌鳥耳原中陵）より先に築かれた可能性が高いと考えられている。大仙陵古墳（百舌鳥耳原中陵）の陪塚として宮内庁により管理されているが、形態や規模が大きいいことから独立の古墳であると考えられている。江戸時代には「長山塚」、「長山」とよばれ、また[[王仁]]や、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の墓とする伝承もある{{Sfn|古墳群|2014|p=14}}。周濠の南側に接して[[大阪府道2号大阪中央環状線]]が周濠の一部をまたぐように通る。 == 規模 == 古墳の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長100メートル * 後円部直径63メートル。高さ10.3メートル。 * 前方部幅68.5メートル。高さ10.3メートル。 == 文化財 == === 堺市指定史跡 === * 指定名称：'''永山古墳周濠''' - 2016年（平成28年）4月11日指定。 == 世界遺産 == [[2019年]]（令和元年）7月6日に世界文化遺産 '''百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-''' を構成する百舌鳥古墳群の構成資産の一部として登録されている{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=世界遺産／文化遺産オンライン|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=文化庁}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=世界遺産／百舌鳥・古市古墳群―古代日本の墳墓群／国指定文化財等データベース|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=文化庁}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/learn/mozu_furuichi_mozu_list.html|title=百舌鳥エリア古墳リスト／百舌鳥・古市古墳群|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=百舌鳥・古市古墳群世界遺産保存活用会議}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 松村隆文 「永山古墳」『日本古墳大辞典』 株式会社東京堂出版 1989年 428項 * {{Cite book|和書|title=百舌鳥古墳群 -堺の文化財- 第7版|year=2014|publisher=堺市文化観光局文化部文化財課|author=堺市文化観光局文化部文化財課 編集|ref={{SfnRef|古墳群|2014}}}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html 永山古墳【世界文化遺産 構成資産】] - 堺市文化観光局博物館学芸課 * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html 百舌鳥古墳群リスト（詳細ページ）] - 堺市文化観光局文化部世界遺産課 * [http://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/spot.php?id=120 スポット詳細：永山古墳｜堺観光ガイド] - （公社）堺観光コンベンション協会 {{デフォルトソート:なかやまこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:堺区の歴史]] [[category:百舌鳥古墳群]] [[category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:陵墓陪冢]]

{{日本の古墳| 名称=永山古墳| 画像=[[ファイル:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
墳丘全景（手前に前方部、右奥に後円部）| 所属=[[百舌鳥古墳群]]| 所在地=大阪府堺市堺区東永山園| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=永山古墳}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 築造年代=5世紀（推定）| 規模=墳丘長100ｍ| 被葬者=| 陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定（「百舌鳥耳原中陵」の陪冢）| 史跡=堺市指定史跡「永山古墳周濠」| 出土品=| 画像||地図2={{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=240|marker=place-of-worship}}{{Location map|Japan Osaka#Japan|float=center}}}} [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|永山古墳の航空写真
{{国土航空写真}}]] '''永山古墳'''（ながやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]東永山園に所在する[[前方後円墳]]。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つで、[[文化遺産 (世界遺産)|世界文化遺産]] [[百舌鳥・古市古墳群|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-]] の構成資産の一部として登録されている。 [[宮内庁]]により第16代[[仁徳天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]の「[[大仙陵古墳|百舌鳥耳原中陵]]」の陪冢に治定されているほか、周濠は堺市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[大仙陵古墳]]（百舌鳥耳原中陵）の北西約50メートルにあり、前方部を南に向けた前方後円墳である{{Sfn|古墳群|2014|p=14}}。墳丘の規模は、長さ100メートル、後円部径63メートル、後円部高さ10.3メートル、前方部幅68.5メートル、前方部高さ10.3メートル。墳丘西側のくびれ部分に造り出しがあり、葺石と埴輪があり{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=永山古墳　【世界文化遺産 構成資産】|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=堺市役所文化観光局博物館学芸課}}、墳丘は2段に見えるが、他の古墳よりも腰高であるため3段築成の可能性もある{{Sfn|古墳群|2014|p=14}}。調査されていないため、構造の詳細は不明で、副葬品の内容や性格も分かっていないが、5世紀前半の築造と考えられる。周囲には濠があり、くびれ部の東側には濠をまたぐ渡り土手があるが、築造当時から存在するものかどうかは不明である{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=永山古墳／国指定文化財等データベース|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=文化庁}}。1928年（昭和3年）に前方部西端の堤上から円筒埴輪（埴輪列の可能性あり）が出土したとされる{{Sfn|古墳群|2014|p=14}}。2012年（平成24年）に管理用柵の設置工事の事前調査が行われ、東側の濠の範囲を確認し、周濠の肩は近世以降に改変を受けたことが判明。また、東側の周濠の堆積層の下層に永山古墳の遺構が良好に残されている可能性があることが確認されている{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/nagayama.html|title=永山古墳周濠|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=堺市役所文化観光局文化部文化財課}}。前方後円墳の墳端および周濠が良好に残され、隣接する大仙陵古墳（百舌鳥耳原中陵）より先に築かれた可能性が高いと考えられている。大仙陵古墳（百舌鳥耳原中陵）の陪塚として宮内庁により管理されているが、形態や規模が大きいいことから独立の古墳であると考えられている。江戸時代には「長山塚」、「長山」とよばれ、また[[王仁]]や、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の墓とする伝承もある{{Sfn|古墳群|2014|p=14}}。周濠の南側に接して[[大阪府道2号大阪中央環状線]]が周濠の一部をまたぐように通る。 == 規模 == 古墳の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長100メートル * 後円部直径63メートル。高さ10.3メートル。 * 前方部幅68.5メートル。高さ10.3メートル。 == 文化財 == === 堺市指定史跡 === * 指定名称：'''永山古墳周濠''' - 2016年（平成28年）4月11日指定。 == 世界遺産 == [[2019年]]（令和元年）7月6日に世界文化遺産 '''百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-''' を構成する百舌鳥古墳群の構成資産の一部として登録されている{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=世界遺産／文化遺産オンライン|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=文化庁}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=世界遺産／百舌鳥・古市古墳群―古代日本の墳墓群／国指定文化財等データベース|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=文化庁}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/learn/mozu_furuichi_mozu_list.html|title=百舌鳥エリア古墳リスト／百舌鳥・古市古墳群|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=百舌鳥・古市古墳群世界遺産保存活用会議}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 松村隆文 「永山古墳」『日本古墳大辞典』 株式会社東京堂出版 1989年 428項 * {{Cite book|和書|title=百舌鳥古墳群 -堺の文化財- 第7版|year=2014|publisher=堺市文化観光局文化部文化財課|author=堺市文化観光局文化部文化財課 編集|ref={{SfnRef|古墳群|2014}}}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html 永山古墳【世界文化遺産 構成資産】] - 堺市文化観光局博物館学芸課 * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html 百舌鳥古墳群リスト（詳細ページ）] - 堺市文化観光局文化部世界遺産課 * [http://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/spot.php?id=120 スポット詳細：永山古墳｜堺観光ガイド] - （公社）堺観光コンベンション協会 {{デフォルトソート:なかやまこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:堺区の歴史]] [[category:百舌鳥古墳群]] [[category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:陵墓陪冢]]

{{日本の古墳| 名称=永山古墳| 画像=[[ファイル:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]

墳丘全景（手前に前方部、右奥に後円部）| 所属=[[百舌鳥古墳群]]| 所在地=大阪府堺市堺区東永山園| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=永山古墳}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 築造年代=5世紀（推定）| 規模=墳丘長100ｍ| 被葬者=| 陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定（「百舌鳥耳原中陵」の陪冢）| 史跡=堺市指定史跡「永山古墳周濠」| 出土品=| 画像||地図2={{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=240|marker=place-of-worship}}{{Location map|Japan Osaka#Japan|float=center}}}} [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|永山古墳の航空写真（2007年）
{{国土航空写真}}]] '''永山古墳'''（ながやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]東永山園に所在する[[前方後円墳]]。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つで、[[文化遺産 (世界遺産)|世界文化遺産]] [[百舌鳥・古市古墳群|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-]] の構成資産の一部として登録されている。 [[宮内庁]]により第16代[[仁徳天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]の「[[大仙陵古墳|百舌鳥耳原中陵]]」の陪冢に治定されているほか、周濠は堺市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[大仙陵古墳]]（百舌鳥耳原中陵）の北西約50メートルにあり、前方部を南に向けた前方後円墳である{{Sfn|古墳群|2014|p=14}}。墳丘の規模は、長さ100メートル、後円部径63メートル、後円部高さ10.3メートル、前方部幅68.5メートル、前方部高さ10.3メートル。墳丘西側のくびれ部分に造り出しがあり、葺石と埴輪があり{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=永山古墳　【世界文化遺産 構成資産】|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=堺市役所文化観光局博物館学芸課}}、墳丘は2段に見えるが、他の古墳よりも腰高であるため3段築成の可能性もある{{Sfn|古墳群|2014|p=14}}。調査されていないため、構造の詳細は不明で、副葬品の内容や性格も分かっていないが、5世紀前半の築造と考えられる。周囲には濠があり、くびれ部の東側には濠をまたぐ渡り土手があるが、築造当時から存在するものかどうかは不明である{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=永山古墳／国指定文化財等データベース|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=文化庁}}。1928年（昭和3年）に前方部西端の堤上から円筒埴輪（埴輪列の可能性あり）が出土したとされる{{Sfn|古墳群|2014|p=14}}。2012年（平成24年）に管理用柵の設置工事の事前調査が行われ、東側の濠の範囲を確認し、周濠の肩は近世以降に改変を受けたことが判明。また、東側の周濠の堆積層の下層に永山古墳の遺構が良好に残されている可能性があることが確認されている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/nagayama.html|title=永山古墳周濠|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=堺市役所文化観光局文化部文化財課}}。前方後円墳の墳端および周濠が良好に残され、隣接する大仙陵古墳（百舌鳥耳原中陵）より先に築かれた可能性が高いと考えられている。大仙陵古墳（百舌鳥耳原中陵）の陪塚として宮内庁により管理されているが、形態や規模が大きいいことから独立の古墳であると考えられている。江戸時代には「長山塚」、「長山」とよばれ、また[[王仁]]や、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の墓とする伝承もある{{Sfn|古墳群|2014|p=14}}。周濠の南側に接して[[大阪府道2号大阪中央環状線]]が周濠の一部をまたぐように通る。 == 規模 == 古墳の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長100メートル * 後円部直径63メートル。高さ10.3メートル。 * 前方部幅68.5メートル。高さ10.3メートル。 == 文化財 == === 堺市指定史跡 === * 指定名称：'''永山古墳周濠''' - 2016年（平成28年）4月11日指定。 == 世界遺産 == [[2019年]]（令和元年）7月6日に世界文化遺産 '''百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-''' を構成する百舌鳥古墳群の構成資産の一部として登録されている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=世界遺産／文化遺産オンライン|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=文化庁}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=世界遺産／百舌鳥・古市古墳群―古代日本の墳墓群／国指定文化財等データベース|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=文化庁}}{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/learn/mozu_furuichi_mozu_list.html|title=百舌鳥エリア古墳リスト／百舌鳥・古市古墳群|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=百舌鳥・古市古墳群世界遺産保存活用会議}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 松村隆文 「永山古墳」『日本古墳大辞典』 株式会社東京堂出版 1989年 428項 * {{Cite book|和書|title=百舌鳥古墳群 -堺の文化財- 第7版|year=2014|publisher=堺市文化観光局文化部文化財課|author=堺市文化観光局文化部文化財課 編集|ref={{SfnRef|古墳群|2014}}}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html 永山古墳【世界文化遺産 構成資産】] - 堺市文化観光局博物館学芸課 * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html 百舌鳥古墳群リスト（詳細ページ）] - 堺市文化観光局文化部世界遺産課 * [http://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/spot.php?id=120 スポット詳細：永山古墳｜堺観光ガイド] - （公社）堺観光コンベンション協会 {{デフォルトソート:なかやまこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:堺区の歴史]] [[category:百舌鳥古墳群]] [[category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:陵墓陪冢]]

View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|na..."
revision.text:
{{Japanese Kofun Infobox |Name = Nagayama Kofun |Image = [[File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagayama Kofun}} |Shape = Keyhole-shaped kofun |Construction = Estimated 5th century |Size = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Imperial Tomb = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |Artifacts = None reported |Image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, registered as part of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}. The tumulus is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}, designated as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a designated historic site by {{ill|Sakai City|ja|堺市}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|accessdate=2021-11-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Tombs]] [[ja:永山古墳]]

{{Japanese Kofun Infobox |Name = Nagayama Kofun |Image = [[File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagayama Kofun}} |Shape = Keyhole-shaped kofun |Construction = Estimated 5th century |Size = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Imperial Tomb = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |Artifacts = None reported |Image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, registered as part of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}. The tumulus is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}, designated as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a designated historic site by {{ill|Sakai City|ja|堺市}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|accessdate=2021-11-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Tombs]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:永山古墳]]

{{Japanese Kofun Infobox |Name = Nagayama Kofun |Image = [[File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagayama Kofun}} |Shape = Keyhole-shaped kofun |Construction = Estimated 5th century |Size = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Imperial Tomb = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |Artifacts = None reported |Image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, registered as part of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}. The tumulus is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}, designated as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a designated historic site by {{ill|Sakai City|ja|堺市}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|accessdate=2021-11-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Imperial Tombs]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]]永山古墳]]

The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:Imperial Tombs]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:Original page]]

{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, registered as part of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}. The tumulus is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}, designated as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a designated historic site by {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|accessdate=2021-11-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:Imperial Tombs]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:kk]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:kk]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:kk]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:kk]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:test if the jawiki is actually present]]

revision.text:
{{Japanese Kofun Infobox |Name = Nagayama Kofun |Image = [[File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagayama Kofun}} |Shape = Keyhole-shaped kofun |Construction = Estimated 5th century |Size = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Imperial Tomb = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |Artifacts = None reported |Image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, registered as part of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}. The tumulus is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}, designated as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a designated historic site by {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|accessdate=2021-11-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Imperial Tombs]] [[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:kk]][[ja:[[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:kk]][[ja:[[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:kk]][[ja:[[Category:kk]][[ja:[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:test if the jawiki is actually present]]

{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, registered as part of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}. The tumulus is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}, designated as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a designated historic site by {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|accessdate=2021-11-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Tombs]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]]

The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Tombs]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:kk]]

{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, registered as part of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}. The tumulus is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}, designated as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a designated historic site by {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|accessdate=2021-11-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Tombs]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:kk]]

revision.text:
{{Japanese Kofun Infobox |Name = Nagayama Kofun |Image = [[File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagayama Kofun}} |Shape = Keyhole-shaped kofun |Construction = Estimated 5th century |Size = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Imperial Tomb = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |Artifacts = None reported |Image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, registered as part of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}. The tumulus is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}, designated as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a designated historic site by {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|accessdate=2021-11-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Tombs]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]]

However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Tombs]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]]

revision.text:
{{Japanese Kofun Infobox |Name = Nagayama Kofun |Image = [[File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagayama Kofun}} |Shape = Keyhole-shaped kofun |Construction = Estimated 5th century |Size = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Imperial Tomb = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |Artifacts = None reported |Image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, registered as part of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}. The tumulus is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}, designated as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a designated historic site by {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|accessdate=2021-11-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Imperial Tombs]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:bump 1-25]]

The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - {{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, registered as part of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}. The tumulus is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}, designated as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a designated historic site by {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|accessdate=2021-11-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - revision.text:
{{Japanese Kofun Infobox |Name = Nagayama Kofun |Image = [[File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagayama Kofun}} |Shape = Keyhole-shaped kofun |Construction = Estimated 5th century |Size = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Imperial Tomb = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |Artifacts = None reported |Image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, registered as part of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}. The tumulus is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}, designated as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a designated historic site by {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|accessdate=2021-11-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Tombs]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nagayama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nagayama Kofun-qq]] [[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nagayama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]]
