World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Tombs]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nagayama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nagayama Kofun-qq]] [[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nagayama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:suQqqqqq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Japanese Kofun Infobox |Name = Nagayama Kofun |Image = [[File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |Location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagayama Kofun}} |Shape = Keyhole-shaped kofun |Construction = Estimated 5th century |Size = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Imperial Tomb = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |Artifacts = None reported |Image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, registered as part of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}. The tumulus is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}, designated as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a designated historic site by {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|accessdate=2021-11-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Tombs]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nagayama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nagayama Kofun-qq]] [[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nagayama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:suQqqqqq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - {{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}, registered as part of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}. The tumulus is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}, designated as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a designated historic site by {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|accessdate=2021-11-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. Its moat is a designated historic site by {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|accessdate=2021-11-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - Its moat is a designated historic site by {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|accessdate=2021-11-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Tombs]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]]

The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|accessdate=2021-11-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - Its moat is a designated historic site by {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies aqqroximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|accessdate=2021-11-14}} The structure aqqears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Tombs]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:qq]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |Name = Nagayama Kofun |Image = [[File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |Location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagayama Kofun}} |Shape = Keyhole-shaped kofun |Construction = Estimated 5th century |Size = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Imperial Tomb = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |Artifacts = None reported |Image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, registered as part of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}. The tumulus is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}, designated as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a designated historic site by {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies aqqroximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|accessdate=2021-11-14}} The structure aqqears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Tombs]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:qq]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. {{Infobox ancient site |Name = Nagayama Kofun |Image = [[File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |Location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagayama Kofun}} |Shape = Keyhole-shaped kofun |Construction = Estimated 5th century |Size = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Imperial Tomb = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |Artifacts = None reported |Image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, registered as part of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}. The tumulus is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}, designated as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a designated historic site by {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies aqqroximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|accessdate=2021-11-14}} The structure aqqears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Tombs]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:qq]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Tombs]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |Name = Nagayama Kofun |Image = [[File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |Location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagayama Kofun}} |Shape = Keyhole-shaped kofun |Construction = Estimated 5th century |Size = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Imperial Tomb = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |Artifacts = None reported |Image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, registered as part of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}. The tumulus is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}, designated as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a designated historic site by {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies [[Category:pp error]]approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|accessdate=2021-11-14}} The structure a11ears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Tombs]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |Name = Nagayama Kofun |image = [[File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |Location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagayama Kofun}} |Shape = Keyhole-shaped kofun |Construction = Estimated 5th century |Size = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Imperial Tomb = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |Artifacts = None reported |Image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, registered as part of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}. The tumulus is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}, designated as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a designated historic site by {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies [[Category:pp error]]approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|accessdate=2021-11-14}} The structure a11ears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Tombs]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |Location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagayama Kofun}} |Shape = Keyhole-shaped kofun |Construction = Estimated 5th century |Size = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Imperial Tomb = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |Artifacts = None reported |image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, registered as part of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}. The tumulus is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}, designated as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a designated historic site by {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies [[Category:pp error]]approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|accessdate=2021-11-14}} The structure a11ears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April 11, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-11-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Tombs]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Tombs]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pp]][[en:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |Name = Nagayama Kofun |image = [[File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |Location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagayama Kofun}} |Shape = Keyhole-shaped kofun |Construction = Estimated 5th century |Size = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Imperial Tomb = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |Artifacts = None reported |image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, registered as part of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}. The tumulus is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}, designated as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a designated historic site by {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies [[Category:pp error]]approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} The structure appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Tombs]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]][[category:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pp]][[en:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "9年" to "9th year "
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |Name = Nagayama Kofun |image = [[File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |Location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagayama Kofun}} |Shape = Keyhole-shaped kofun |Construction = Estimated 5th century |Size = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Imperial Tomb = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |burial goods = None reported |image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, registered as part of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}. The tumulus is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}, designated as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a designated historic site by {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies [[Category:pp error]]approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} The structure appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019th year }}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |Name = Nagayama Kofun |image = [[File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |Location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagayama Kofun}} |Shape = Keyhole-shaped kofun |Construction = Estimated 5th century |Size = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Imperial Tomb = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |burial goods = None reported |image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, registered as part of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-}}. The tumulus is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}, designated as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a designated historic site by {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies [[Category:pp error]]approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} The structure appear[[Category:pp to pp]]s to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or occupant remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == Dimensions == The tumulus' dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Cultural Heritage == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019th year }}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Cultural Heritage Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Cultural Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Cultural Heritage|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Cultural Heritage Component] (Sakai City Cultural Heritage Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]]
