revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pp to pp]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nagayama Kofun |image = [[File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) | part_of = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu Tombs|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai-ku|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagayama Kofun}} |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun |built = Estimated 5th century |area = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Mausoleum = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |burial goods = None reported |image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai-ku|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu Tombs|qq=}}, registered as part of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}. The tumulus is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=}}, Historic Site1 as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a Historic Site1 historic site by {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai|qq=}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or Interred_individual remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == area == The tumulus' area are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019|qq=}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Nagayama Kofun]] }} [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[en:Nagayama Kofun]]

The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == area == The tumulus' area are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019|qq=}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[en:Nagayama Kofun]]

revision.comment: Bot: mark page as missing translated-page template
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nagayama Kofun |image = [[File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) | part_of = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu Tombs|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai-ku|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagayama Kofun}} |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun |built = Estimated 5th century |area = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Mausoleum = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |burial goods = None reported |image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai-ku|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu Tombs|qq=}}, registered as part of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}. The tumulus is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=}}, Historic Site1 as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a Historic Site1 historic site by {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai|qq=}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or Interred_individual remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == area == The tumulus' area are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019|qq=}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[en:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

The tumulus is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=}}, Historic Site1 as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a Historic Site1 historic site by {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai|qq=}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or Interred_individual remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == area == The tumulus' area are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019|qq=}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[en:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakai-ku|ja|堺区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai-ku|qq=}}, {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. Its moat is a Historic Site1 historic site by {{ill|Sakai|ja|堺市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai|qq=}}. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or Interred_individual remain unknown. height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In {{ill|2019|ja|2019年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2019|qq=}}, the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[en:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) | part_of = [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] |location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|qq=}}, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagayama Kofun}} |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun |built = Estimated 5th century |area = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Mausoleum = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |burial goods = None reported |image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|qq=}}, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], registered as part of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}. The tumulus is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=}}, Historic Site1 as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a Historic Site1 historic site by [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or Interred_individual remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == area == The tumulus' area are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In [[:en:2019|2019]], the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[en:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In [[:en:2019|2019]], the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nagayama Kofun |image = [[File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) | part_of = [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] |location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|qq=}}, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagayama Kofun}} |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun |built = Estimated 5th century |area = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Mausoleum = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |burial goods = None reported |image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|qq=}}, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], registered as part of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}. The tumulus is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=}}, Historic Site1 as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a Historic Site1 historic site by [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or Interred_individual remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == area == The tumulus' area are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In [[:en:2019|2019]], the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka]] [[Category:History of Sakai-ku]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nagayama Kofun |image = [[File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) | part_of = [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] |location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagayama Kofun}} |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun |built = Estimated 5th century |area = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Mausoleum = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |burial goods = None reported |image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], registered as part of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The tumulus is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, Historic Site1 as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a Historic Site1 historic site by [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or Interred_individual remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == area == The tumulus' area are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In [[:en:2019|2019]], the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]]

The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or Interred_individual remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == area == The tumulus' area are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In [[:en:2019|2019]], the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] }} [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]]

{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], registered as part of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The tumulus is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, Historic Site1 as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a Historic Site1 historic site by [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. revision.comment: Text replacement - "<!-- no {{translated page}} template found in history -->" to "<!-- no translated page template found in history -->"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nagayama Kofun |image = [[File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) | part_of = [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] |location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagayama Kofun}} |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun |built = Estimated 5th century |area = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Mausoleum = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |burial goods = None reported |image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], registered as part of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The tumulus is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, Historic Site1 as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a Historic Site1 historic site by [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or Interred_individual remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == area == The tumulus' area are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In [[:en:2019|2019]], the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] }} [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]]

Its moat is a Historic Site1 historic site by [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or Interred_individual remain unknown. revision.comment: 25 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:永山古墳 up to rev 98981723
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=永山古墳| 画像=[[ファイル:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
墳丘全景（手前に前方部、右奥に後円部）| 所属=[[百舌鳥古墳群]]| 所在地=大阪府堺市堺区東永山園| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=永山古墳}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 築造年代=5世紀（推定）| 規模=墳丘長100ｍ| 被葬者=| 陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定（「百舌鳥耳原中陵」の陪冢）| 史跡=堺市指定史跡「永山古墳周濠」| 出土品=| 画像||地図2={{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=240|marker=place-of-worship}}{{Location map|Japan Osaka#Japan|float=center}}}} [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|永山古墳の航空写真（2007年）
{{国土航空写真}}]] '''永山古墳'''（ながやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]東永山園に所在する[[前方後円墳]]。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つで、[[文化遺産 (世界遺産)|世界文化遺産]] [[百舌鳥・古市古墳群|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-]] の構成資産の一部として登録されている。 [[宮内庁]]により第16代[[仁徳天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]の「[[大仙陵古墳|百舌鳥耳原中陵]]」の陪冢に治定されているほか、周濠は堺市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[大仙陵古墳]]（百舌鳥耳原中陵）の北西約50メートルにあり、前方部を南に向けた前方後円墳である{{Sfn|古墳群|2014|p=14}}。墳丘の規模は、長さ100メートル、後円部径63メートル、後円部高さ10.3メートル、前方部幅68.5メートル、前方部高さ10.3メートル。墳丘西側のくびれ部分に造り出しがあり、葺石と埴輪があり{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=永山古墳　【世界文化遺産 構成資産】|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=堺市役所文化観光局博物館学芸課}}、墳丘は2段に見えるが、他の古墳よりも腰高であるため3段築成の可能性もある{{Sfn|古墳群|2014|p=14}}。調査されていないため、構造の詳細は不明で、副葬品の内容や性格も分かっていないが、5世紀前半の築造と考えられる。周囲には濠があり、くびれ部の東側には濠をまたぐ渡り土手があるが、築造当時から存在するものかどうかは不明である{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=永山古墳／国指定文化財等データベース|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=文化庁}}。1928年（昭和3年）に前方部西端の堤上から円筒埴輪（埴輪列の可能性あり）が出土したとされる{{Sfn|古墳群|2014|p=14}}。2012年（平成24年）に管理用柵の設置工事の事前調査が行われ、東側の濠の範囲を確認し、周濠の肩は近世以降に改変を受けたことが判明。また、東側の周濠の堆積層の下層に永山古墳の遺構が良好に残されている可能性があることが確認されている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/nagayama.html|title=永山古墳周濠|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=堺市役所文化観光局文化部文化財課}}。前方後円墳の墳端および周濠が良好に残され、隣接する大仙陵古墳（百舌鳥耳原中陵）より先に築かれた可能性が高いと考えられている。大仙陵古墳（百舌鳥耳原中陵）の陪塚として宮内庁により管理されているが、形態や規模が大きいいことから独立の古墳であると考えられている。江戸時代には「長山塚」、「長山」とよばれ、また[[王仁]]や、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の墓とする伝承もある{{Sfn|古墳群|2014|p=14}}。周濠の南側に接して[[大阪府道2号大阪中央環状線]]が周濠の一部をまたぐように通る。 == 規模 == 古墳の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長100メートル * 後円部直径63メートル。高さ10.3メートル。 * 前方部幅68.5メートル。高さ10.3メートル。 == 文化財 == === 堺市指定史跡 === * 指定名称：'''永山古墳周濠''' - 2016年（平成28年）4月11日指定。 == 世界遺産 == [[2019年]]（令和元年）7月6日に世界文化遺産 '''百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-''' を構成する百舌鳥古墳群の構成資産の一部として登録されている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=世界遺産／文化遺産オンライン|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=文化庁}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=世界遺産／百舌鳥・古市古墳群―古代日本の墳墓群／国指定文化財等データベース|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=文化庁}}{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/learn/mozu_furuichi_mozu_list.html|title=百舌鳥エリア古墳リスト／百舌鳥・古市古墳群|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=百舌鳥・古市古墳群世界遺産保存活用会議}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 松村隆文 「永山古墳」『日本古墳大辞典』 株式会社東京堂出版 1989年 428頁 * {{Cite book|和書|title=百舌鳥古墳群 -堺の文化財- 第7版|year=2014|publisher=堺市文化観光局文化部文化財課|author=堺市文化観光局文化部文化財課 編集|ref={{SfnRef|古墳群|2014}}}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html 永山古墳【世界文化遺産 構成資産】] - 堺市文化観光局博物館学芸課 * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html 百舌鳥古墳群リスト（詳細ページ）] - 堺市文化観光局文化部世界遺産課 * [http://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/spot.php?id=120 スポット詳細：永山古墳｜堺観光ガイド] - （公社）堺観光コンベンション協会 {{デフォルトソート:なかやまこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:堺区の歴史]] [[category:百舌鳥古墳群]] [[category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:陵墓陪冢]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[永山古墳]] to [[Nagayama Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=永山古墳| 画像=[[ファイル:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
墳丘全景（手前に前方部、右奥に後円部）| 所属=[[百舌鳥古墳群]]| 所在地=大阪府堺市堺区東永山園| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=永山古墳}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 築造年代=5世紀（推定）| 規模=墳丘長100ｍ| 被葬者=| 陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定（「百舌鳥耳原中陵」の陪冢）| 史跡=堺市指定史跡「永山古墳周濠」| 出土品=| 画像||地図2={{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=240|marker=place-of-worship}}{{Location map|Japan Osaka#Japan|float=center}}}} [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|永山古墳の航空写真（2007年）
{{国土航空写真}}]] '''永山古墳'''（ながやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]東永山園に所在する[[前方後円墳]]。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つで、[[文化遺産 (世界遺産)|世界文化遺産]] [[百舌鳥・古市古墳群|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-]] の構成資産の一部として登録されている。 [[宮内庁]]により第16代[[仁徳天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]の「[[大仙陵古墳|百舌鳥耳原中陵]]」の陪冢に治定されているほか、周濠は堺市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[大仙陵古墳]]（百舌鳥耳原中陵）の北西約50メートルにあり、前方部を南に向けた前方後円墳である{{Sfn|古墳群|2014|p=14}}。墳丘の規模は、長さ100メートル、後円部径63メートル、後円部高さ10.3メートル、前方部幅68.5メートル、前方部高さ10.3メートル。墳丘西側のくびれ部分に造り出しがあり、葺石と埴輪があり{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=永山古墳　【世界文化遺産 構成資産】|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=堺市役所文化観光局博物館学芸課}}、墳丘は2段に見えるが、他の古墳よりも腰高であるため3段築成の可能性もある{{Sfn|古墳群|2014|p=14}}。調査されていないため、構造の詳細は不明で、副葬品の内容や性格も分かっていないが、5世紀前半の築造と考えられる。周囲には濠があり、くびれ部の東側には濠をまたぐ渡り土手があるが、築造当時から存在するものかどうかは不明である{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/911/00000141|title=永山古墳／国指定文化財等データベース|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=文化庁}}。1928年（昭和3年）に前方部西端の堤上から円筒埴輪（埴輪列の可能性あり）が出土したとされる{{Sfn|古墳群|2014|p=14}}。2012年（平成24年）に管理用柵の設置工事の事前調査が行われ、東側の濠の範囲を確認し、周濠の肩は近世以降に改変を受けたことが判明。また、東側の周濠の堆積層の下層に永山古墳の遺構が良好に残されている可能性があることが確認されている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/nagayama.html|title=永山古墳周濠|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=堺市役所文化観光局文化部文化財課}}。前方後円墳の墳端および周濠が良好に残され、隣接する大仙陵古墳（百舌鳥耳原中陵）より先に築かれた可能性が高いと考えられている。大仙陵古墳（百舌鳥耳原中陵）の陪塚として宮内庁により管理されているが、形態や規模が大きいいことから独立の古墳であると考えられている。江戸時代には「長山塚」、「長山」とよばれ、また[[王仁]]や、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の墓とする伝承もある{{Sfn|古墳群|2014|p=14}}。周濠の南側に接して[[大阪府道2号大阪中央環状線]]が周濠の一部をまたぐように通る。 == 規模 == 古墳の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長100メートル * 後円部直径63メートル。高さ10.3メートル。 * 前方部幅68.5メートル。高さ10.3メートル。 == 文化財 == === 堺市指定史跡 === * 指定名称：'''永山古墳周濠''' - 2016年（平成28年）4月11日指定。 == 世界遺産 == [[2019年]]（令和元年）7月6日に世界文化遺産 '''百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-''' を構成する百舌鳥古墳群の構成資産の一部として登録されている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=世界遺産／文化遺産オンライン|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=文化庁}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=世界遺産／百舌鳥・古市古墳群―古代日本の墳墓群／国指定文化財等データベース|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=文化庁}}{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/learn/mozu_furuichi_mozu_list.html|title=百舌鳥エリア古墳リスト／百舌鳥・古市古墳群|accessdate=2021.11.14|publisher=百舌鳥・古市古墳群世界遺産保存活用会議}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 松村隆文 「永山古墳」『日本古墳大辞典』 株式会社東京堂出版 1989年 428頁 * {{Cite book|和書|title=百舌鳥古墳群 -堺の文化財- 第7版|year=2014|publisher=堺市文化観光局文化部文化財課|author=堺市文化観光局文化部文化財課 編集|ref={{SfnRef|古墳群|2014}}}} == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html 永山古墳【世界文化遺産 構成資産】] - 堺市文化観光局博物館学芸課 * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html 百舌鳥古墳群リスト（詳細ページ）] - 堺市文化観光局文化部世界遺産課 * [http://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/spot.php?id=120 スポット詳細：永山古墳｜堺観光ガイド] - （公社）堺観光コンベンション協会 {{デフォルトソート:なかやまこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:堺区の歴史]] [[category:百舌鳥古墳群]] [[category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:陵墓陪冢]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nagayama Kofun |image = [[File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) | part_of = [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] |location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagayama Kofun}} |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun |built = Estimated 5th century |area = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Mausoleum = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |burial goods = None reported |image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], registered as part of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The tumulus is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, Historic Site1 as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a Historic Site1 historic site by [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or Interred_individual remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == area == The tumulus' area are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In [[:en:2019|2019]], the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] }} [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:永山古墳]] {{translated page|ja|永山古墳|version=98981723|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], registered as part of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The tumulus is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, Historic Site1 as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a Historic Site1 historic site by [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or Interred_individual remain unknown. height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In [[:en:2019|2019]], the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] }} [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:永山古墳]] {{translated page|ja|永山古墳|version=98981723|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:堺区の歴史]]
