But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nagayama Kofun |image = [[File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) | part_of = [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] |location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagayama Kofun}} |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun |built = Estimated 5th century |area = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Mausoleum = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |burial goods = None reported |image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], registered as part of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The tumulus is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, Historic Site1 as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a Historic Site1 historic site by [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or Interred_individual remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == area == The tumulus' area are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In [[:en:2019|2019]], the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] }} [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:永山古墳]] {{translated page|ja|永山古墳|version=98981723|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:堺区の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]]

{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], registered as part of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The tumulus is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, Historic Site1 as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a Historic Site1 historic site by [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or Interred_individual remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == area == The tumulus' area are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In [[:en:2019|2019]], the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] }} [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:永山古墳]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|永山古墳|version=98981723|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:堺区の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:百舌鳥古墳群]] [[Category:陵墓陪冢]]

The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or Interred_individual remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == area == The tumulus' area are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In [[:en:2019|2019]], the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] }} [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:永山古墳]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|永山古墳|version=98981723|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:堺区の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:百舌鳥古墳群]] [[Category:陵墓陪冢]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nagayama Kofun |image = [[File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) | part_of = [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] |location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagayama Kofun}} |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun |built = Estimated 5th century |area = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Mausoleum = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |burial goods = None reported |image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], registered as part of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The tumulus is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, Historic Site1 as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a Historic Site1 historic site by [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or Interred_individual remain unknown. height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In [[:en:2019|2019]], the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] }} [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[ja:永山古墳]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|永山古墳|version=98981723|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Sakai Ward]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:百舌鳥古墳群]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea and attendant mounds]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]]

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] into [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] via bot
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nagayama Kofun |image = [[File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|280px]]
View of the tumulus (foreground: front mound, background: rear circular mound) | part_of = [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] |location = {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|6|N|135|29|13|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nagayama Kofun}} |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun |built = Estimated 5th century |area = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Mausoleum = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |burial goods = None reported |image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], registered as part of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The tumulus is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, Historic Site1 as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a Historic Site1 historic site by [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or Interred_individual remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == area == The tumulus' area are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In [[:en:2019|2019]], the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] }} [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[ja:永山古墳]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|永山古墳|version=98981723|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Sakai Ward]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:百舌鳥古墳群]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea and attendant mounds]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]]

revision.comment: Bot: sync infobox from Wikidata (P18,P131,P625,P112,P149,P547,P361,P580)
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11549996]] Wikidata name: Nagayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nagayama Kofun |image = File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG | part_of = {{ill|Mozu Tombs|qid=Q4250584}} |location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|qid=Q1042008}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.568|N|135.4867|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun |built = Estimated 5th century |area = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Mausoleum = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |burial goods = None reported |image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718}} }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], registered as part of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The tumulus is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, Historic Site1 as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a Historic Site1 historic site by [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or Interred_individual remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == area == The tumulus' area are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In [[:en:2019|2019]], the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] }} [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]] [[ja:永山古墳]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|永山古墳|version=98981723|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Sakai Ward]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea and attendant mounds]] [[sn:Nagayama Kofun]]

It is one of the tumuli that comprise the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], registered as part of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its moat is a Historic Site1 historic site by [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. {{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], registered as part of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The tumulus is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, Historic Site1 as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a Historic Site1 historic site by [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or Interred_individual remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == area == The tumulus' area are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In [[:en:2019|2019]], the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:永山古墳]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|永山古墳|version=98981723|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Sakai Ward]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea and attendant mounds]] {{wikidata link|Q11549996}}

It is one of the tumuli that comprise the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], registered as part of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The tumulus is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, Historic Site1 as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a Historic Site1 historic site by [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or Interred_individual remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == area == The tumulus' area are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In [[:en:2019|2019]], the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:永山古墳]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|永山古墳|version=98981723|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Sakai-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea and attendant mounds]] {{wikidata link|Q11549996}}

height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In [[:en:2019|2019]], the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:永山古墳]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|永山古墳|version=98981723|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Sakai-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea and attendant mounds]] {{wikidata link|Q11549996}}

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[ja:永山古墳]][[zh:永山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11549996]] Wikidata name: Nagayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nagayama Kofun |image = File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG | part_of = {{ill|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584}} |location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|WD=Q1042008}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.568|N|135.4867|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun |built = Estimated 5th century |area = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Mausoleum = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |burial goods = None reported |image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], registered as part of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The tumulus is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, Historic Site1 as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a Historic Site1 historic site by [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or Interred_individual remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == area == The tumulus' area are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In [[:en:2019|2019]], the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:永山古墳]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|永山古墳|version=98981723|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Sakai-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea and attendant mounds]] {{wikidata link|Q11549996}}

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Nagayama Kofun]][[ja:永山古墳]][[zh:永山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11549996]] Wikidata name: Nagayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name = Nagayama Kofun |image = File:Nagayama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG | part_of = {{ill|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584}} |location = {{ill|Sakai-ku|WD=Q1042008}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.568|N|135.4867|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = Keyhole-shaped kofun |built = Estimated 5th century |area = Mound length: 100 m |Occupant = Unknown |Mausoleum = Designated as a "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi" (陪冢) by the Imperial Household Agency |Historic Site = Designated as a Sakai City Historic Site: "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" |burial goods = None reported |image 2 = [[File:Nagayama Tomb Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Nagayama Kofun (2007)
{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], registered as part of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The tumulus is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, Historic Site1 as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a Historic Site1 historic site by [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or Interred_individual remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == area == The tumulus' area are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In [[:en:2019|2019]], the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:永山古墳]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|永山古墳|version=98981723|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Sakai-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea and attendant mounds]] {{wikidata link|Q11549996}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], registered as part of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The tumulus is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, Historic Site1 as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a Historic Site1 historic site by [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or Interred_individual remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == area == The tumulus' area are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In [[:en:2019|2019]], the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:永山古墳]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|永山古墳|version=98981723|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Sakai-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea and attendant mounds]] {{wikidata link|Q11549996}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], registered as part of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The tumulus is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, Historic Site1 as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a Historic Site1 historic site by [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or Interred_individual remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == area == The tumulus' area are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In [[:en:2019|2019]], the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:永山古墳]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|永山古墳|version=98981723|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Sakai-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea and attendant mounds]] {{wikidata link|Q11549996}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

The tumulus is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as an associated mound (陪冢) of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, Historic Site1 as "Mozu Mimihara Naka no Misasagi." Its moat is a Historic Site1 historic site by [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]]. == Overview == The Nagayama Kofun lies approximately 50 meters northwest of {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisenryō Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, oriented with its front portion facing south. The mound is 100 meters in total length, with a circular rear mound diameter of 63 meters and height of 10.3 meters, and a front mound width of 68.5 meters and height of 10.3 meters.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html|title=Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} The structure appears to be two-tiered, but its proportions suggest it may have been built in three tiers.{{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} A moat encircles the kofun, with an earthen bridge crossing its eastern section. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or Interred_individual remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == area == The tumulus' area are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters; height: 10.3 meters == Historic Site == === Sakai City Historic Site === * Designated as the "Moat of Nagayama Kofun" on April pp, 2016. == World Heritage Status == In [[:en:2019|2019]], the Nagayama Kofun was registered as a component of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Historic Site: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|author=Matsumura Takafumi|pages=428}} * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster: Sakai's Historic Site|publisher=Sakai City Historic Site Division|year=2014}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nagayama.html Nagayama Kofun - World Historic Site Component] (Sakai City Historic Site Division) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/kohun_siru/mozukofungunrist-shousai.html Mozu Kofun Cluster Detailed List] (Sakai City World Heritage Division) {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleums]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:永山古墳]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|永山古墳|version=98981723|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Sakai-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea and attendant mounds]] {{wikidata link|Q11549996}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

{{Aerial Photography of National Land}}]] |architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Nagayama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|永山古墳}}) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in {{ill|Higashi Nagayama-en|ja|東永山園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Nagayama-en|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Sakai-ku, Sakai|Sakai-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is one of the tumuli that comprise the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]], registered as part of the UNESCO World Historic Site Site {{ill|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. However, whether this feature dates back to the tumulus' original construction remains unclear.{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails/9pp/00000141|title=Nagayama Kofun - National Cultural Property Database|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|accessdate=2021-pp-14}} Excavations have not been conducted, and details about the internal structure, burial goods, or Interred_individual remain unknown. The kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 5th century. == area == The tumulus' area are as follows: * Total length: 100 meters * Rear circular mound diameter: 63 meters; height: 10.3 meters * Front mound width: 68.5 meters;
